g.text: Deep Learning Quantization: Integer Overflow Analysis
1. Calibrate scale to cover max expected weight
2. Always use clamp() before casting to int8
4. Account for Zero-Point in asymmetric quantization

text.tspan: # CORRECT: Clamp to valid INT8 range [-128, 127]
# CORRECT: Clamp to valid INT8 range [-128, 127]

div.p: The Intermediate Overflow: INT8 × INT8 max = 127² = 16,129. A dot product of length 1024 could reach: 1024 × 16,129 = 16.5 Million. Requires INT32 Accumulator!

text.tspan: # Kernel performs i8 * i8 -> i32 sum
# Requantize i32 back to i8 only at the end
# Kernel performs i8 * i8 -> i32 sum
# Requantize i32 back to i8 only at the end
